title: Declare MCP outputSchema + structured content on server tools
source: MCP spec 2025-06-18 (structured tool output / outputSchema) — knowledge cutoff Jan 2026, live search unavailable this run; verify at modelcontextprotocol.io/specification
description: mcp_server.py tools appear to return free-form text/JSON strings. The MCP spec added `outputSchema` on tool definitions plus a `structuredContent` field on tool results, so clients can validate and machine-consume responses instead of re-parsing prose. Adding output schemas to board/project_status/list_tickets/get_ticket makes downstream agents (and the Rick Terminal) consume typed data reliably and lets Claude self-correct malformed tool responses.
proposed_changes: For each read tool in mcp_server.py, define a JSON Schema in schemas.py and attach it as the tool's `outputSchema`. Return results with both a text content block (backward compat) and a `structuredContent` object matching the schema. Start with project_status, board, list_tickets, get_ticket, prometheus_status. Add a small validation helper that asserts payloads conform before returning.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve delegation failed: (no stderr)
